Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Dr. Mehta / Anand Mehta correspondence using search_emails (query: "Mehta" or "Anand Mehta"). Expected findings: (1) Fatimah's welcome email to Dr. Mehta laying out the three workstreams (housing, equipment, household move), CC'd to Mina Hashimoto and Patricia Langford; (2) Dr. Mehta's detailed reply with equipment inventory (2x Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors S/N RX660-1147A and RX660-1203A, SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet ~350 lbs with patient research data, Herman Miller Aeron chair, Uplift V2 desk); (3) Fatimah's email forwarding three UrbanNest housing options to Dr. Mehta (Option A River North $1,250/wk, Option B Streeterville $1,350/wk recommended, Option C Gold Coast $1,450/wk) with a hold deadline; (4) critically, NO reply from Dr. Mehta selecting a housing option the hold deadline was end of day April 25 and it is now April 26, meaning the 48-hour holds have expired and housing selection is overdue.
OE 2: Search emails for Kovacs / BorderEase customs correspondence using search_emails (query: "Kovacs" or "BorderEase" or "BSF186" or "customs"). Expected findings: emails related to Dr. Renata Kovacs's Nashville-to-Toronto international cross-border move (April 14–17), including Fatimah's coordination with BorderEase Customs for the CBSA BSF186 Personal Effects declaration, the employer letter request from Patricia Langford, and status of the customs filing. The agent needs to determine whether BorderEase completed the filing and whether the move itself concluded without issues.
OE 3: Search emails for Nadia Okonjo / Lorraine Pham / USCIS correspondence using search_emails (query: "Okonjo" or "Lorraine Pham" or "USCIS" or "visa" or "H-1B"). Expected findings: emails related to Nadia Okonjo's San Francisco-to-Austin move (April 14–18) for PivotPoint Labs, including coordination with immigration attorney Lorraine Pham (Pham & Associates, lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) regarding the USCIS address-change filing required for her H-1B visa (expires June 15, 2026). The agent needs to determine whether the attorney confirmed the USCIS filing was handled before the physical move occurred.
OE 4: Search Slack messages for Mehta relocation updates using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mehta" or "Canopy Health executive") or by browsing the operations channel (C006) history via conversations_history. Expected findings: (1) Mina Hashimoto's initial alert about the high-priority CMO relocation request from Patricia Langford (in C006); (2) Chloe Vance's involvement in structuring the billing scope and workstreams (visible in her C005 estimate request to Marcus Thorne), with Fatimah subsequently confirmed as coordinator through the C006 thread and email trail; (3) Fatimah's confirmation that Linear tickets are updated; (4) Marcus Thorne's QuickBooks estimate confirmation (EST-2026-0422, $12,170) note: this message is in C005 (#finance), discoverable via conversations_search_messages but not via C006 history; (5) Fatimah's consolidated status update (posted April 24) showing: Workstream 1 (Housing) options presented, awaiting Dr. Mehta's selection, deadline end of day Friday April 25 before 48-hour holds expire; Workstream 2 (Equipment) inventory confirmed, chain-of-custody template still needed from Canopy compliance (blocker); Workstream 3 (Household Move) pending housing confirmation to determine Chicago delivery address, proposed pack date April 28 via Swift Relocations.
OE 5: Search Linear for existing Mehta relocation tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta") or by browsing the team_relocations or proj_canopy project. Expected findings: three existing tickets all assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi and marked In Progress with Priority 1 (Urgent): (1) Issue #343 "Temporary Housing Dr. Anand Mehta Chicago River North (Canopy Health)" (due May 1) housing options presented, awaiting selection; (2) Issue #375 "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody Dr. Mehta Medical Research Equipment" (due April 30) equipment inventory confirmed but chain-of-custody documentation template still needed from Canopy compliance; (3) Issue #396 "Household Move Dr. Anand Mehta Boston to Chicago (Canopy Health)" (due May 3) pending, will engage Swift once housing is confirmed. Also check linear_list_comments on these issues for any status updates note that the UrbanNest housing options, pricing details, and the April 25 hold deadline are primarily documented in the email trail (Fatimah's forwarded options to Dr. Mehta) and Fatimah's Slack consolidated status update in C006, not in Linear comments.
OE 6: Search or list Airtable Relocations records for Kovacs and Okonjo using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Dr. Renata Kovacs") and similarly for "Nadia Okonjo". Expected findings: (1) Dr. Renata Kovacs record (recKovacsNashvilleToronto) Nashville to Toronto, move dates April 14–17, Assigned Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Status: "In Progress" but the move dates have passed (it is now April 26), so status should be "Completed" if the move went through; (2) Nadia Okonjo record (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin) San Francisco to Austin, move dates April 14–18, Assigned Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Status: "In Progress" move dates have also passed, status should be "Completed" if confirmed. Both records need status updates.
OE 7: Search Airtable Relocations records for Dr. Mehta using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Mehta" or "Dr. Anand Mehta"). Expected finding: no existing Airtable relocation record for Dr. Mehta. The CRM deal and Linear tickets exist, but the Relocations table has not yet been updated with a record for this move. This is a gap that should be addressed when updating relocation records.
OE 8: Search CRM for Mehta relocation deal and Canopy Health account using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Mehta") or crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health") and crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]). Expected findings: (1) Deal "Canopy Health Dr. Anand Mehta Executive Relocation (Boston to Chicago, May 2026)" amount $12,170, stage In Progress, close date May 7, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Account Manager Mina Hashimoto; (2) CRM engagement from Mina (April 23) recording the new deal, contact, and QuickBooks estimate; (3) Canopy Health company record showing it is MoveOps's longest-standing client (since July 2024) and this is their first C-suite placement.
OE 9: Search CRM for Kovacs and Okonjo deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Kovacs") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "Okonjo" or dealname: "PivotPoint"). Expected findings: (1) Kovacs deal (deal_canopy_kovacs_apr2026) $3,850, stage In Progress, vendor Swift Relocations, customs broker BorderEase Customs; (2) Okonjo deal (deal_pivot_april_2026_dual) PivotPoint Labs dual relocation (Okonjo + Castañeda), $10,650, stage In Progress, H-1B visa coordination noted.
OE 10: Look up contact information for Patricia Langford and Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Expected findings: Patricia Langford Director of HR at Canopy Health, email patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io; Mina Hashimoto Account Manager at MoveOps, email mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. These are needed as the recipient and CC for the outgoing status email.
OE 11: Search emails or Slack for Swift Relocations packing date confirmation for Mehta using search_emails (query: "Swift" and "Mehta" or "packing" and "Boston") or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Swift Mehta packing"). Expected finding: the proposed timeline from Fatimah's Slack update shows Swift packing crew is planned for April 28 in Boston, but formal confirmation from Swift (Greg Pallone, greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) has not yet been received packing dates are not yet locked in. The household move via Swift is pending until housing is confirmed (to determine Chicago delivery address).
OE 12: Search emails or calendar for Mehta flight booking status using search_emails (query: "flight" and "Mehta") or calendar_search_events (query: "Mehta" or "flight Boston Chicago"). Expected finding: the QuickBooks estimate includes a $370 line item for flight booking with premium cabin upgrade (2 passengers Dr. Mehta and partner Priya), but no flight has been booked yet. Based on the overall timeline (pack April 28, transit April 29–30, delivery May 1, start date May 5), the flight would need to be booked for approximately May 2–3, but no booking has been finalized.
OE 13: Create Linear tracking ticket(s) for overdue or at-risk Mehta items using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations", project: "proj_canopy", priority: 1). At minimum, create a ticket flagging the housing selection as overdue the 48-hour hold on the UrbanNest options expired at end of day April 25, Dr. Mehta has not responded, and new options may need to be sourced or holds re-extended with UrbanNest. Additional at-risk items that may warrant tickets: chain-of-custody documentation template still not obtained from Canopy compliance (blocker for equipment packing, due April 30), Swift packing dates not confirmed (proposed April 28 but not locked in), flight not yet booked (May 2–3 target). These tickets should be assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi so Chloe Vance has visibility through the Linear board.
OE 14: Update Airtable relocation record for Dr. Renata Kovacs to "Completed" using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recKovacsNashvilleToronto", fields: {"Status": "Completed"}}]). The move dates were April 14–17 and it is now April 26 the physical relocation is finished. The prompt specifically instructs to fix the status if records still show "In Progress."
OE 15: Update Airtable relocation record for Nadia Okonjo to "Completed" using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin", fields: {"Status": "Completed"}}]). The move dates were April 14–18 and it is now April 26. The prompt instructs to confirm the attorney filing went through and then close out the record. Status should change from "In Progress" to "Completed" once the Lorraine Pham USCIS filing is confirmed.
OE 16: Create an Airtable relocation record for Dr. Anand Mehta using airtable_create_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", fields: {Name: "Dr. Anand Mehta", Company: "Canopy Health", Status: "In Progress", Origin: "Boston, MA", Destination: "Chicago, IL", Move Start Date: TBD based on Swift scheduling, Move End Date: TBD, Assigned Coordinator: "Fatimah Al-Rashidi", Special Requirements: noting three workstreams, executive VIP relocation, chain-of-custody, pregnant partner}). This fills the gap there is no existing relocation record for Mehta in Airtable despite active CRM deal and Linear tickets.
OE 17: Send email from Fatimah to Patricia Langford at Canopy about the Mehta relocation status using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io"], cc: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], subject: regarding Dr. Mehta relocation status update, content: covering the current state of all three workstreams housing options presented but selection overdue, equipment inventory confirmed but chain-of-custody template still needed from Canopy compliance, household move planned via Swift pending housing confirmation and packing date lock-in, flight not yet booked and what still needs to happen before the May 5 on-site deadline).
OE 18: Post a summary message in the MoveOps operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: consolidated summary of where the Mehta relocation stands, what has been confirmed, what is overdue or at risk, and what needs to happen in the next nine days before the May 5 on-site deadline. Should also mention the Kovacs and Okonjo record updates for team awareness).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Dr. Mehta / Anand Mehta correspondence using search_emails (query: "Mehta" or "Anand Mehta"). Expected findings: (1) Fatimah's welcome email to Dr. Mehta laying out the three workstreams (housing, equipment, household move), CC'd to Mina Hashimoto and Patricia Langford; (2) Dr. Mehta's detailed reply with equipment inventory (2x Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors S/N RX660-1147A and RX660-1203A, SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet ~350 lbs with patient research data, Herman Miller Aeron chair, Uplift V2 desk); (3) Fatimah's email forwarding three UrbanNest housing options to Dr. Mehta (Option A River North $1,250/wk, Option B Streeterville $1,350/wk recommended, Option C Gold Coast $1,450/wk) with a hold deadline; (4) critically, NO reply from Dr. Mehta selecting a housing option the hold deadline was end of day April 25 and it is now April 26, meaning the 48-hour holds have expired and housing selection is overdue.
OE 2: Search emails for Kovacs / BorderEase customs correspondence using search_emails (query: "Kovacs" or "BorderEase" or "BSF186" or "customs"). Expected findings: emails related to Dr. Renata Kovacs's Nashville-to-Toronto international cross-border move (April 14–17), including Fatimah's coordination with BorderEase Customs for the CBSA BSF186 Personal Effects declaration, the employer letter request from Patricia Langford, and status of the customs filing. The agent needs to determine whether BorderEase completed the filing and whether the move itself concluded without issues.
OE 3: Search emails for Nadia Okonjo / Lorraine Pham / USCIS correspondence using search_emails (query: "Okonjo" or "Lorraine Pham" or "USCIS" or "visa" or "H-1B"). Expected findings: emails related to Nadia Okonjo's San Francisco-to-Austin move (April 14–18) for PivotPoint Labs, including coordination with immigration attorney Lorraine Pham (Pham & Associates, lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) regarding the USCIS address-change filing required for her H-1B visa (expires June 15, 2026). The agent needs to determine whether the attorney confirmed the USCIS filing was handled before the physical move occurred.
OE 4: Search Slack messages for Mehta relocation updates using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mehta" or "Canopy Health executive") or by browsing the operations channel (C006) history via conversations_history. Expected findings: (1) Mina Hashimoto's initial alert about the high-priority CMO relocation request from Patricia Langford (in C006); (2) Chloe Vance's involvement in structuring the billing scope and workstreams (visible in her C005 estimate request to Marcus Thorne), with Fatimah subsequently confirmed as coordinator through the C006 thread and email trail; (3) Fatimah's confirmation that Linear tickets are updated; (4) Marcus Thorne's QuickBooks estimate confirmation (EST-2026-0422, $12,170) note: this message is in C005 (#finance), discoverable via conversations_search_messages but not via C006 history; (5) Fatimah's consolidated status update (posted April 24) showing: Workstream 1 (Housing) options presented, awaiting Dr. Mehta's selection, deadline end of day Friday April 25 before 48-hour holds expire; Workstream 2 (Equipment) inventory confirmed, chain-of-custody template still needed from Canopy compliance (blocker); Workstream 3 (Household Move) pending housing confirmation to determine Chicago delivery address, proposed pack date April 28 via Swift Relocations.
OE 5: Search Linear for existing Mehta relocation tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta") or by browsing the team_relocations or proj_canopy project. Expected findings: three existing tickets all assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi and marked In Progress with Priority 1 (Urgent): (1) Issue #343 "Temporary Housing Dr. Anand Mehta Chicago River North (Canopy Health)" (due May 1) housing options presented, awaiting selection; (2) Issue #375 "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody Dr. Mehta Medical Research Equipment" (due April 30) equipment inventory confirmed but chain-of-custody documentation template still needed from Canopy compliance; (3) Issue #396 "Household Move Dr. Anand Mehta Boston to Chicago (Canopy Health)" (due May 3) pending, will engage Swift once housing is confirmed. Also check linear_list_comments on these issues for any status updates note that the UrbanNest housing options, pricing details, and the April 25 hold deadline are primarily documented in the email trail (Fatimah's forwarded options to Dr. Mehta) and Fatimah's Slack consolidated status update in C006, not in Linear comments.
OE 6: Search or list Airtable Relocations records for Kovacs and Okonjo using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Dr. Renata Kovacs") and similarly for "Nadia Okonjo". Expected findings: (1) Dr. Renata Kovacs record (recKovacsNashvilleToronto) Nashville to Toronto, move dates April 14–17, Assigned Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Status: "In Progress" but the move dates have passed (it is now April 26), so status should be "Completed" if the move went through; (2) Nadia Okonjo record (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin) San Francisco to Austin, move dates April 14–18, Assigned Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Status: "In Progress" move dates have also passed, status should be "Completed" if confirmed. Both records need status updates.
OE 7: Search Airtable Relocations records for Dr. Mehta using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Mehta" or "Dr. Anand Mehta"). Expected finding: no existing Airtable relocation record for Dr. Mehta. The CRM deal and Linear tickets exist, but the Relocations table has not yet been updated with a record for this move. This is a gap that should be addressed when updating relocation records.
OE 8: Search CRM for Mehta relocation deal and Canopy Health account using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Mehta") or crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health") and crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]). Expected findings: (1) Deal "Canopy Health Dr. Anand Mehta Executive Relocation (Boston to Chicago, May 2026)" amount $12,170, stage In Progress, close date May 7, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Account Manager Mina Hashimoto; (2) CRM engagement from Mina (April 23) recording the new deal, contact, and QuickBooks estimate; (3) Canopy Health company record showing it is MoveOps's longest-standing client (since July 2024) and this is their first C-suite placement.
OE 9: Search CRM for Kovacs and Okonjo deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Kovacs") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "Okonjo" or dealname: "PivotPoint"). Expected findings: (1) Kovacs deal (deal_canopy_kovacs_apr2026) $3,850, stage In Progress, vendor Swift Relocations, customs broker BorderEase Customs; (2) Okonjo deal (deal_pivot_april_2026_dual) PivotPoint Labs dual relocation (Okonjo + Castañeda), $10,650, stage In Progress, H-1B visa coordination noted.
OE 10: Look up contact information for Patricia Langford and Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Expected findings: Patricia Langford Director of HR at Canopy Health, email patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io; Mina Hashimoto Account Manager at MoveOps, email mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. These are needed as the recipient and CC for the outgoing status email.
OE 11: Search emails or Slack for Swift Relocations packing date confirmation for Mehta using search_emails (query: "Swift" and "Mehta" or "packing" and "Boston") or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Swift Mehta packing"). Expected finding: the proposed timeline from Fatimah's Slack update shows Swift packing crew is planned for April 28 in Boston, but formal confirmation from Swift (Greg Pallone, greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) has not yet been received packing dates are not yet locked in. The household move via Swift is pending until housing is confirmed (to determine Chicago delivery address).
OE 12: Search emails or calendar for Mehta flight booking status using search_emails (query: "flight" and "Mehta") or calendar_search_events (query: "Mehta" or "flight Boston Chicago"). Expected finding: the QuickBooks estimate includes a $370 line item for flight booking with premium cabin upgrade (2 passengers Dr. Mehta and partner Priya), but no flight has been booked yet. Based on the overall timeline (pack April 28, transit April 29–30, delivery May 1, start date May 5), the flight would need to be booked for approximately May 2–3, but no booking has been finalized.
OE 13: Create Linear tracking ticket(s) for overdue or at-risk Mehta items using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations", project: "proj_canopy", priority: 1). At minimum, create a ticket flagging the housing selection as overdue the 48-hour hold on the UrbanNest options expired at end of day April 25, Dr. Mehta has not responded, and new options may need to be sourced or holds re-extended with UrbanNest. Additional at-risk items that may warrant tickets: chain-of-custody documentation template still not obtained from Canopy compliance (blocker for equipment packing, due April 30), Swift packing dates not confirmed (proposed April 28 but not locked in), flight not yet booked (May 2–3 target). These tickets should be assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi so Chloe Vance has visibility through the Linear board.
OE 14: Update Airtable relocation record for Dr. Renata Kovacs to "Completed" using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recKovacsNashvilleToronto", fields: {"Status": "Completed"}}]). The move dates were April 14–17 and it is now April 26 the physical relocation is finished. The prompt specifically instructs to fix the status if records still show "In Progress."
OE 15: Update Airtable relocation record for Nadia Okonjo to "Completed" using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin", fields: {"Status": "Completed"}}]). The move dates were April 14–18 and it is now April 26. The prompt instructs to confirm the attorney filing went through and then close out the record. Status should change from "In Progress" to "Completed" once the Lorraine Pham USCIS filing is confirmed.
OE 16: Create an Airtable relocation record for Dr. Anand Mehta using airtable_create_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", fields: {Name: "Dr. Anand Mehta", Company: "Canopy Health", Status: "In Progress", Origin: "Boston, MA", Destination: "Chicago, IL", Move Start Date: TBD based on Swift scheduling, Move End Date: TBD, Assigned Coordinator: "Fatimah Al-Rashidi", Special Requirements: noting three workstreams, executive VIP relocation, chain-of-custody, pregnant partner}). This fills the gap there is no existing relocation record for Mehta in Airtable despite active CRM deal and Linear tickets.
OE 17: Send email from Fatimah to Patricia Langford at Canopy about the Mehta relocation status using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io"], cc: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], subject: regarding Dr. Mehta relocation status update, content: covering the current state of all three workstreams housing options presented but selection overdue, equipment inventory confirmed but chain-of-custody template still needed from Canopy compliance, household move planned via Swift pending housing confirmation and packing date lock-in, flight not yet booked and what still needs to happen before the May 5 on-site deadline).
OE 18: Post a summary message in the MoveOps operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: consolidated summary of where the Mehta relocation stands, what has been confirmed, what is overdue or at risk, and what needs to happen in the next nine days before the May 5 on-site deadline. Should also mention the Kovacs and Okonjo record updates for team awareness).
